core.sustainability.calculator module

SUSTAINABILITY CALCULATOR – Module 2 Main Entry Point

Requirements Covered:

Req 2.1: Country-Specific Carbon Intensity Req 2.2: Country-Specific Water Intensity Req 2.3: Methane Leakage Factor Req 2.4: Total Operational Carbon (CCI) Req 2.5: Total Water Consumption (WCI) Req 2.6: Methane Emission Impact (MCI) Req 2.7: Energy Per Query (EPQ) Req 2.8: Wait-Tax Per Query (TPQ) Req 2.9: Carbon Receipt Per Task Req 2.10: Water Receipt Per Task Req 2.12: Geographic Arbitrage Potential Req 2.13: Energy Scarcity Index (ESI) Req 2.16: Global Metadata Integrity

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.sustainability.calculator.SustainabilityCalculator(config_loader)[source]

Bases: object

Main sustainability calculator.

Transforms energy measurements into environmental impacts using country-specific grid factors from Module 0.

Requirements:

Req 2.1-2.3: Grid intensity factors Req 2.4-2.6: Environmental impacts Req 2.7-2.10: Per-query metrics Req 2.12-2.13: Geographic context Req 2.16: Data integrity

calculate_arbitrage(energy_kwh: float, current_country: str = 'US') → Dict[str, Any][source]

Calculate potential savings by running in different grid regions.

Parameters:

  • energy_kwh – Energy consumption in kWh

  • current_country – Current grid region

Returns:

Dictionary with best country and potential savings

calculate_from_derived(derived: DerivedEnergyMeasurement, country_code: str = 'US', query_count: int = 1) → SustainabilityResult[source]

Calculate sustainability from derived measurement.

This uses workload energy instead of total package energy, giving a more accurate picture of the environmental impact of the actual workload.

Parameters:

  • derived – DerivedEnergyMeasurement from Module 1 analyzer

  • country_code – ISO country code for grid factors [Req 2.1-2.3]

  • query_count – Number of queries for per-query normalization [Req 2.7-2.10]

Returns:

SustainabilityResult object

calculate_from_raw(raw: RawEnergyMeasurement, country_code: str = 'US', query_count: int = 1) → SustainabilityResult[source]

Calculate sustainability from raw measurement.

Parameters:

  • raw – RawEnergyMeasurement from Module 1

  • country_code – ISO country code for grid factors [Req 2.1-2.3]

  • query_count – Number of queries for per-query normalization [Req 2.7-2.10]

Returns:

SustainabilityResult object

calculate_scarcity_index(energy_kwh: float, country_code: str = 'US') → float[source]

Calculate Energy Scarcity Index (ESI).

ESI = energy_kwh / household_daily_kwh

Parameters:

  • energy_kwh – Energy consumption in kWh

  • country_code – ISO country code

Returns:

ESI value (ratio of experiment energy to household daily)

list_countries() → list[source]

Get list of available countries.

class core.sustainability.calculator.SustainabilityResult(measurement_id: str, country_code: str, carbon: CarbonMetrics, water: WaterMetrics, methane: MethaneMetrics, energy_kwh: float, timestamp: float, energy_j: float = 0.0, carbon_intensity_g_per_kwh: float = 0.0, water_intensity_l_per_kwh: float = 0.0, methane_intensity_g_per_kwh: float = 0.0)[source]

Bases: object

Complete sustainability results for a measurement.

measurement_id

Reference to raw measurement

Type:

str

country_code

Grid region used

Type:

str

carbon

CarbonMetrics object

Type:

core.sustainability.models.carbon_metrics.CarbonMetrics

water

WaterMetrics object

Type:

core.sustainability.models.water_metrics.WaterMetrics

methane

MethaneMetrics object

Type:

core.sustainability.models.methane_metrics.MethaneMetrics

energy_j

Total energy in joules (NEW)

Type:

float

energy_kwh

Total energy in kilowatt-hours

Type:

float

carbon_intensity_g_per_kwh

Grid carbon intensity used (NEW)

Type:

float

water_intensity_l_per_kwh

Grid water intensity used (NEW)

Type:

float

methane_intensity_g_per_kwh

Grid methane intensity used (NEW)

Type:

float

timestamp

When calculation was performed

Type:

float

carbon_intensity_g_per_kwh: float = 0.0

energy_j: float = 0.0

methane_intensity_g_per_kwh: float = 0.0

Human-readable summary.

timestamp: float

Convert to dictionary for serialization.
